jobs.description: To help us process your application efficiently, please start your email subject line with [CAA] We are building a stylized anime action RPG in Unreal Engine (PC + Mobile, crossplay). For a 15 minute Gamescom demo (target: August), we are producing a small set of high impact in game cinematics using Level Sequence / Sequencer. We are looking for a Remote Unreal Cinematic Artist (Freelance) who can set up shots in Unreal, build strong camera language, assemble sequences, and create clean gameplay handovers. Responsibilities Build and maintain Level Sequences and shot structure (shots, sub sequences, takes, versioning). Set up and animate cameras: framing, lens choices, camera movement, shake, focus, and pacing. Assemble imported animation assets into sequencers, handle timing and retiming, and maintain continuity. Collaborate with the Cinematic Director on cut decisions, rhythm, and cinematic language. Work with the Lead Animator to ensure animation readability and camera alignment. Support gameplay integration: triggers, safe handover back to gameplay camera, state restore, and sequencing constraints. Coordinate with VFX and Lighting for shot polish and consistency. Troubleshoot common Sequencer issues: bindings, possessables vs spawnables, animation tracks, transforms, and evaluation. jobs.title: A UX/UI Designer at Artech
jobs.description: Company Description Artech is the 10th Largest IT Staffing Company in the US, according to Staffing Industry Analysts' 2012 annual report. Artech provides technical expertise to fill gaps in clients' immediate skill-sets availability, deliver emerging technology skill-sets, refresh existing skill base, allow for flexibility in project planning and execution phases, and provide budgeting/financial flexibility by offering contingent labor as a variable cost. Job Description Responsible to research and apply interaction design principals to develop effective UX design strategies. They will contribute to the team’s overall understanding of product relevance and user behavior. UX/UI designer will work with various stakeholders on an ongoing basis to produce concepts, sketches, wireframes, user flows and comps. Responsible to prepare production files, documentations and specs for developers. This person will be responsible for the visual design for Omni channel program. The Omni channel program is taking our customer facing flows and empowering agents in our retail stores and call centers to use them to assist customers in a store or over the phone. The goal is to make a flow work for both the majority of the time. Some things you'd work on: Collaborate with designers and leads on existing patterns for our responsive websites across e-commerce and self service. Collaborate with Omni channel design lead on functionality and overall flow. Utilize design patterns when possible to concept new features while following core business requirements. Maintain up-to-date comps and InVision prototypes as requirements evolve. Work with development to implement designs. This may include light production work when needed. Work with design leads to understand business requirements and tech limitations. Note: this role is less about up front research and discovery and more about the nuts and bolts of visual design and UI design. Experience: Articulate rationale a must. Sketch and InVision

We’re proud to have a diverse, global team where we value the individual and enrich our culture by considering varied perspectives because we believe people power progress. Progress is seeking a Principal UX Designer to define and drive the design vision for Progress Agentic RAG , ensuring exceptional user experiences. This role combines deep expertise in user experience design with hands-on knowledge of front-end technologies. As a design leader, you will guide product teams in delivering intuitive, scalable, and accessible experiences that delight users and drive business outcomes. Your ability to bridge the gap between design and development will be key to creating seamless collaboration between UX, product, and engineering teams.
